description_long: This is a vintage manual coffee grinder with a square wooden base and a metal grinding mechanism on top. The base is box-shaped, featuring a small drawer at the front for collecting ground coffee, and is topped with a flat, slightly rusted metal plate. The grinding mechanism consists of a round, dome-shaped metal hopper with a lid, and a long, horizontal metal crank handle with a rounded knob for turning. The metal components show signs of age and oxidation, giving the grinder a rustic, well-used appearance. The drawer at the front is fitted with a small knob for easy pulling, and the overall construction suggests durability and functionality. The object is compact, likely designed for tabletop use in kitchens, cafes, or urban markets.
description: A vintage manual coffee grinder with a wooden box base, metal grinding mechanism, and a long crank handle. The object has a rustic, aged appearance with a front drawer for collecting grounds.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square wooden base, flat metal top, and the small drawer with a knob at the bottom center.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long horizontal crank handle extending to the left and the dome-shaped metal hopper on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden box and the rounded metal hopper, with the handle mostly hidden.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the crank handle extending to the right, showing the side of the wooden box and the metal top.
attribute_handle_design: A single long, straight metal crank handle with a rounded knob at the end, mounted horizontally on top of the grinder. The handle appears simple, without visible ergonomic features or textured grips.
